media-press.quote:
If Melania Trump had done book illustrations for a book that did the exact same thing, how would media cover it? What if she had done this for a book with anti-black commentary? And then what if Trump called that criticism “reprehensible” how would media react?


Josie Stratman (@JosieStratman)

May

In summation, I don’t believe modern American media is capable of covering the United States as a force for good in the world and that seems like a hugely seismic reality that will continue to have massive negative implications on all stories in the country.

The Japanese attacking Pearl Harbor seems like it would be hard to cover with America as the bad guy, but two immediate anti-US stories would emerge: 1. America staged the attack conspiracy. 2. Japan was justified in the attack because of US colonial imperialism.

How would modern American media cover World War II? I ask because I think two things are true: 1. Modern media is unable to cover good & evil because they believe America is largely evil therefore her foes can’t be. 2. Identity politics have replaced individual responsibility.
